differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune encephalitis (e.g., anti-LGI1 or anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis)
Medication-induced delirium (e.g., from topiramate or acute benzodiazepine withdrawal)
next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture for CSF analysis (cell count, protein, glucose, autoimmune encephalitis panel, paraneoplastic panel, and infectious studies)
Send serum autoimmune and paraneoplastic encephalitis panels
Perform whole-body PET/CT or CT chest/abdomen/pelvis to screen for occult malignancy
Obtain continuous video-EEG monitoring to rule out ongoing nonconvulsive status epilepticus during agitated states
Consider empiric treatment with intravenous methylprednisolone or intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) if autoimmune etiology is highly suspected while awaiting test results
Consult psychiatry for management of severe agitation and delusional psychosis
Review medication list to exclude drug-drug interactions or adverse reactions exacerbating encephalopathy
